title: Where Weavings Teach Each Other

features.name: the loom teaching
features.desc-on-touch: You watch thread find thread in rows that learn each other's tension. Each line is a sentence; the loom hums its lesson into the quiet.

features.name: thread convergence
features.desc-on-touch: Fibres spiral inward where patterns teach new patterns. The archive records how weft answers warp before they meet.

features.name: instruction glyph
features.desc-on-touch: A plaque of thread-marks shows you: this is how weavings have always been teaching each other. The marks are older than names.

features.name: pattern memory
features.desc-on-touch: A stack of threadcount records, each one a lesson from an earlier weaving. The archive has been filing these for longer than looms have existed.

inscription: here the threads never finished learning; the loom keeps teaching because the weft remembers what the warp has already forgotten

file.name: ThreadTeaching_ConvergencePattern_1840sMethod.txt
file.note: Threadcount records and loom-teaching methods: how patterns taught new patterns before language.

exits.label: deeper into the loom chambers where spindles dream their patterns
upward to where the oldest thread still answers
